result.markdown: --- title: "Week 5: Exponential smoothing" --- ::: {.cell} ::: ## What you will learn this week * Simple exponential smoothing and corresponding ETS models * Methods with trend and corresponding ETS models * ETS model notation ## Pre-class activities Read [Sections 8.1-8.4 of the textbook](https://otexts.com/fpp3/expsmooth.html) and watch all embedded videos ## Slides for seminar Download pdf ## [Workshop activities](activities.qmd) ## Seminar Code ::: {.callout appearance="minimal"} [week5-code.R](week5-code.R){download="week5-code.R"} ::: ## Tutorial exercises ::: {.callout-note} ## Tutorial Learning Objectives 1. **Implement and interpret exponential smoothing models**. - Able to estimate simple exponential smoothing (SES) model, interpret the smoothing parameters, and generate point and interval forecasts. 2. **Develop custom forecasting functions**. - Write and test user-defined functions to perform simple exponential smoothing, compare their outputs with built-in functions (like ETS()), and understand the computational steps involved. 3. **Optimise model parameters using numerical methods**. - Modify exponential smoothing functions to compute an error measure and use numerical optimisation methods to determine the optimal smoothing parameter - Able to compare results with the automatic ETS estimation. ::: - Complete Exercises 1-4 from [Section 8.8](https://otexts.com/fpp3/expsmooth-exercises.html). ## Assignments * [IA2](../assignments/A2.qmd) is due on Thursday 02 April. * [GA2](../assignments/G2.qmd) is due on Tuesday 14 April. * [IA3](../assignments/A3.qmd) is due on Friday 24 April. ## Weekly quiz * Week 5 quiz: [ETF3231](https://learning.monash.edu/mod/quiz/view.php?id=5400133) | [ETF5231](https://learning.monash.edu/mod/quiz/view.php?id=5400387) — Due Wednesday 01 April.
